Hoe langer jy wag om ’n faktuur te produseer, hoe minder waarskynlik is dit dat jy stiptelike betaling sal ontvang. Om jou debiteure te bestuur, is ook ’n uitstekende manier om geld dop te hou wanneer dit tyd is om belasting te liasseer. En weens hierdie feite is die boekstawing van debiteure een van die belangrikste aspekte van jou besigheidsbestuur.

Gesaghebbende Bronne:

https://www.educba.com/accounts-receivable-journal-entry/

https://www.lawinsider.com/dictionary/net-book-value-of-accounts-receivable

posts.title: How to qualify for short term loans for businesses in South Africa in 2024
posts.excerpt: In need of a short-term loan for your business but don’t know where to start? Applying for short term loans for businesses requires not only one’s financial documentation to be prepared prior to the meeting but also a convincing plan on how you intend to use and pay back the loan. Planning to succeed during ... Read more
posts.desc: Applying for short term loans for businesses requires financial documentation & a convincing plan on how you intend to use and pay back the loan.
posts.html:
In need of a short-term loan for your business but don’t know where to start?

Applying for short term loans for businesses requires not only one’s financial documentation to be prepared prior to the meeting but also a convincing plan on how you intend to use and pay back the loan. Planning to succeed during the negotiation phases increases your likelihood of qualifying for the loan.

 Here are a few requirements you will need to account for before applying for short-term loans for your growing South African business:

  • · Proof of business ownership
  • · Minimum running time of business
  • · Minimum annual revenue
  • · Credit score rating

Different types of loans and means of repayment

Three of the common short term loans for businesses include term loans, invoice financing, and line of credit.

Term loans

With term loans, you usually are allocated a set amount of funds, with a fixed payment and interest rate. The terms are set with the lender in case of late or missed payments. This type of loan is the most familiar and would probably be what you’re considering when applying for a loan. There are, however, more options.

Invoice financing 

In the case of your accounts receivable (AR) not met, you request this loan. The amount is based on the sum total of your invoices and thus allows you to continue work despite unreceived payments. Invoices are thus your collateral.

Business line of credit

What credit would imply, with a fixed pre-determined limit. One repays interest on the funds withdrawn with a flexible arrangement. 

Repayment schemes depending on your loan

When it comes to paying back your loan, there are two main categories; secured or unsecured term loans. Depending on the size of your business and cash flow, this choice drastically impacts the future of many businesses. Repaying short term loans for businesses promptly can be the deciding factor in future relations with the sponsor and cost savings due to interest. Let’s dive into each category.

Secured loan

Invoice financing is an example. Collateral is offered in the case of defaulting on the loan. Having insufficient collateral could potentially disqualify your application. If your cash flow is low, this may be a better option. 

Unsecured loan

Credit ratings play a bigger factor since there isn’t collateral. Ratings directly impact the offers on short term loans for businesses and their repayments. 

Where can I apply for a loan?

For longer-term loans and waiting periods and larger funds, the bank is your go-to. 

Although an independent lender may be a flexible option, there may be unforeseen risks. Sometimes it’s the safer bet as your negotiation skills play the biggest role.

Venture capital or grants are reliable short term loans for businesses with good credit scores.

Applying for the loan

The whole process is ultimately a negotiation. Planning for success involves certain aspects such as assessing your resources and possible lenders down to the engagement. 

Financial statements

Along with your proof of ownership and your financial documentation, owners will look into cash flow. Most often their risk assessment will be unfavourable in low cash flow situations. 

Expectations of numbers: Finding data that aligns with the business plan

Doing your homework on your market size or niche and the likely trends that will play out will help your business regardless of whether you apply for a loan. Keeping tabs on your flux in annual revenue is advised.

Consulting your business analyst and chartered accountant will help you understand financial trends. Providing insight to the bankers will show them your level of commitment. If not, you may be a risk in their eyes. 

Negotiation 

And though it may sound easy, applying them when you are in the midst of a deal-breaker can become quite stressful. Securing short term loans for businesses requires the above to be in place. Preparation determines success. When you engage, it is important to build on your needs incrementally, while assessing the lender’s. A quick tip: try synchronizing your body language with theirs. Negotiating the terms of repayments is an option. Also, remember to record everything in writing. 

Avoid this!

The likelihood of you receiving a loan is undermined when you have been blacklisted or have bad credit. If you don’t find yourself in this situation it is in your best interest to aim for a personal credit score above 700. 

Bad credit score

Credit bureaus will have a record of your business score and bad credit will follow you throughout.

Keeping up to date with your repayments upon receiving a loan can positively impact your finances, amongst other risk factors. Avoiding new debt as well as making sure you’ve paid off old debt is key. 

Having a qualified accountant on retainer will ensure you have a plan in place and the allocated funds necessary. In a situation where you lack cash flow, accountants that have experience with multiple businesses will often provide uncanny solutions by drawing from experience.

af.title: Hoe om te kwalifiseer vir korttermynlenings vir besighede in Suid-Afrika in 2024
af.desc: Aansoek doen vir korttermynlenings vir besighede vereis finansiele dokumentasie en ’n oortuigende plan oor hoe jy die lening gaan gebruik en terugbetaal.
af.html:
Het jy ’n korttermynlening vir jou besigheid nodig, maar weet nie waar om te begin nie?

Aansoek doen vir korttermynlenings vir besighede vereis nie net dat jou finansiele dokumentasie voor die vergadering gereed moet wees nie, maar ook ’n oortuigende plan oor hoe jy van plan is om die lening te gebruik en terug te betaal. Beplanning om suksesvol te wees tydens die onderhandelingsfases verhoog jou kans om vir die lening te kwalifiseer.

 Hier is ’n paar vereistes waarvoor jy voorsiening sal moet maak voordat jy aansoek doen vir korttermynlenings vir jou groeiende Suid-Afrikaanse besigheid:

  • · Bewys van besigheidseienaarskap
  • · Minimum bedryfstyd van die besigheid
  • · Minimum jaarlikse inkomste
  • · Kredietgraderingstelling

Verskillende tipes lenings en wyses van terugbetaling

Drie van die algemene korttermynlenings vir besighede sluit in termynlenings, faktuurfinansiering en ’n kredietlyn.

Termynlenings

Met termynlenings word jou gewoonlik ’n vasgestelde bedrag fondse toegeken, met ’n vaste paaiement en rentekoers. Die voorwaardes word met die lener vasgestel in die geval van laat of gemiste betalings. Hierdie tipe lening is die bekendste en sal waarskynlik wees wat jy oorweeg wanneer jy vir ’n lening aansoek doen. Daar is egter meer opsies.

Faktuurfinansiering 

In die geval dat jou debiteure (AR) nie betaal word nie, versoek jy hierdie lening. Die bedrag is gebaseer op die totale som van jou fakture en laat jou dus toe om voort te gaan met werk ten spyte van onontvangde betalings. Fakture is dus jou sekuriteit.

Besigheidskredietlyn

Wat krediet sou impliseer, met ’n vaste vooraf bepaalde limiet. ’n Mens betaal rente op die fondse wat onttrek word met ’n buigsame reëling. 

Terugbetalingskemas afhangende van jou lening

Wanneer dit kom by die terugbetaling van jou lening, is daar twee hoofkategorieë; verseker of onverseker termynlenings. Afhangende van die grootte van jou besigheid en kontantvloei, het hierdie keuse ’n drastiese impak op die toekoms van baie besighede. Om korttermynlenings vir besighede stiptelik terug te betaal kan die beslissende faktor wees in toekomstige verhoudings met die borg en kostebesparings as gevolg van rente. Kom ons kyk na elke kategorie.

Versekerde lening

Faktuurfinansiering is ’n voorbeeld. Sekuriteit word aangebied in die geval van wanbetaling op die lening. Onvoldoende sekuriteit kan jou aansoek moontlik diskwalifiseer. As jou kontantvloei laag is, kan dit ’n beter opsie wees. 

Onversekerde lening

Kredietgraderings speel ’n groter rol aangesien daar geen sekuriteit is nie. Graderings beïnvloed die aanbiedinge op korttermynlenings vir besighede en hul terugbetalings direk. 

Waar kan ek vir ’n lening aansoek doen?

Vir langertermynlenings en wagtydperke en groter fondse is die bank jou eerste keuse. 

Alhoewel ’n onafhanklike lener ’n buigsame opsie kan wees, kan daar onvoorsiene risiko’s wees. Soms is dit die veiliger keuse aangesien jou onderhandelingsvaardighede die grootste rol speel.

Waagkapitaal of toelaes is betroubare korttermynlenings vir besighede met goeie kredietgraderings.

Aansoek doen vir die lening

Die hele proses is uiteindelik ’n onderhandeling. Beplanning vir sukses behels sekere aspekte soos die assessering van jou hulpbronne en moontlike leners tot by die betrokkenheid. 

Finansiele state

Saam met jou bewys van eienaarskap en jou finansiele dokumentasie sal eienaars na kontantvloei kyk. Heel dikwels sal hul risiko-assessering ongunstig wees in lae kontantvloei-situasies. 

Verwagtinge van syfers: Om data te vind wat ooreenstem met die besigheidsplan

Om jou huiswerk te doen oor jou markgrootte of nis en die waarskynlike tendense wat sal afspeel, sal jou besigheid help, ongeag of jy vir ’n lening aansoek doen. Dit word aangeraai om ’n oog te hou op die wisseling in jou jaarlikse inkomste.

Om jou besigheidsontleder en geoktrooieerde rekenmeester te raadpleeg sal jou help om finansiele tendense te verstaan. Om insig aan die bankiers te verskaf sal hulle jou vlak van toewyding wys. Indien nie, kan jy in hul oë ’n risiko wees. 

Onderhandeling 

En alhoewel dit maklik mag klink, kan dit redelik stresvol word om dit toe te pas wanneer jy in die middel van ’n breekpunt is. Om korttermynlenings vir besighede te bekom vereis dat die bogenoemde in plek is. Voorbereiding bepaal sukses. Wanneer jy betrokke raak, is dit belangrik om inkrementeel op jou behoeftes voort te bou, terwyl jy die lener s’n assesseer. ’n Vinnige wenk: probeer om jou lyftaal met hulle s’n te sinchroniseer. Om die voorwaardes van terugbetalings te onderhandel is ’n opsie. Onthou ook om alles op skrif te stel. 

Vermy dit!

Die waarskynlikheid dat jy ’n lening sal ontvang word ondermyn wanneer jy op die swartlys geplaas is of slegte krediet het. As jy nie in hierdie situasie is nie, is dit in jou beste belang om te mik vir ’n persoonlike kredietgradering bo 700. 

Slegte kredietgradering

Kredietburo’s sal ’n rekord hê van jou besigheidsgradering en slegte krediet sal jou deurgaans volg.

Om op datum te bly met jou terugbetalings nadat jy ’n lening ontvang het kan jou finansies positief beïnvloed, onder andere risikofaktore. Om nuwe skuld te vermy sowel as om seker te maak jy het ou skuld afbetaal is van kardinale belang. 

Om ’n gekwalifiseerde rekenmeester op kontrak te hê sal verseker dat jy ’n plan in plek het en die toegekende fondse wat nodig is. In ’n situasie waar jy kontantvloei kortkom, sal rekenmeesters wat ondervinding met verskeie besighede het dikwels merkwaardige oplossings verskaf deur uit ervaring te put.

posts.title: Hiring an accountant for small business taxes
posts.excerpt: Filing taxes for your small business can be time-consuming. And, while you might gain a thorough knowledge of your financial situation and are prepared to work overtime when it comes to tax season, odds are it’s unsustainable.  Sometimes you might think giving your tax responsibilities to a chartered accountant will solve every problem, but you ... Read more
posts.desc: Hiring an accountant for the filing of your small business taxes is cheaper and they will also file your taxes in a timely manner.
posts.html:
Filing taxes for your small business can be time-consuming. And, while you might gain a thorough knowledge of your financial situation and are prepared to work overtime when it comes to tax season, odds are it’s unsustainable. 

Sometimes you might think giving your tax responsibilities to a chartered accountant will solve every problem, but you neglect to compare their rate to your everyday accountant. Hiring an accountant will allow you to receive reports whenever it is necessary at a cheaper rate. They will also file your taxes in a timely manner. 

Part-time accountant

Maybe your budget or needs require only partial services of an accountant. Hiring a part-time accountant means lower retainer fees. If you’re not planning to use their services all year round then outsourcing is your best option. You may need a dedicated bookkeeper and only call upon your tax advisor come tax season. Small business taxes require accurate bookkeeping independent of your accountant. 

Part-time accountants understand small businesses need to cut down their taxes, and they know when and how to apply regulations in their favour. Don’t know if you qualify for tax relief? They know your situation and how to maximise it.

Accountants provide additional benefits such as planning accounting strategies and inevitably have experience working in a team.

What to consider when hiring an accountant for your taxes

 If you’re not acquainted with or don’t have referrals when hiring a tax accountant, here are a few questions to consider when hiring your first business accountant to gauge their possible level of contribution to your business.

Will they assist you beyond your tax necessities?

Tax season is when they’re most needed, but their role impacts you when it comes to auditing too (depending on your PI score). Are they available out of tax season? If they don’t have a full schedule year-round, are they at least the type of person that would proactively keep up to date with the changes in tax laws?  

Availability: what’s their schedule?

Can you call on them multiple times a year? A month? Whenever you’re in need of financial advice? Are they more interested in helping higher-paying clients instead of managing your small business taxes on a consistent basis? Such details are better known before engaging them for your accounting needs.

Level of experience in relation to your tax needs?

Availability is one thing, but prior experience with similar businesses can mean finding relevant write-offs and tax liability before deadlines pass. Are they well-versed, but not where you need their problem-solving skills? Find an accountant with relevant experience by interviewing more than one.

With the right accountant helping you with your small business taxes, you can expect certain benefits such as: 

Tax Compliance

Like every other enterprise, small business taxes are treated the same. You still have the same responsibilities, not to mention the same resources, too. Compiling and submitting your annual tax statements are made more efficient with eFiling. Keeping around accountants that understand taxes can contribute to your business’s deductions and decrease the workload for the accounting department. 

Do you know whether your small or medium-sized business qualifies for turnover tax (TOT)? The difference in your annual profit could be affected tremendously if you’re paying both income tax and VAT as opposed to paying TOT. Hiring an accountant on such matters could bring your financial standing up a few notches. They could also institute efficient tax processes for the next employee. Restructuring isn’t the easiest task, but with an intuitive mind versed in small business taxes, you will have guided yourself onto the right path. 

Whilst you could do your annual tax returns online, hiring an accountant will simplify the process and effortlessly keep you compliant with governing tax regulations.

Advisory in tax returns and deductions

The number one job of your account is to save you money on your small business taxes. They know where to apply for exemptions, what to register as annual tax deductions, and increase your overall cash flow as a result of filing accurate tax returns. 

Knowing what deductions need to be made can be overwhelming, but having an accountant to help you make decisions (like choosing between a standardized deduction or an itemized deduction) is invaluable. As seasoned veterans in tax deductions, they can help decrease your small business taxes significantly.

Their work is made easier with well-documented financial records managed by a bookkeeper. The two would often work hand in hand. 

Certain professional accountants like chartered accountants or enrolled agents can also advise you on your small business taxes. Both are educated in tax breaks and all matters pertaining to tax as mentioned. While you would rely on your accountant for other small business tax services, you would benefit more from in-depth consulting from specialised accountants.  With an increase in profits, refunds, and exemptions you’re able to increase cash flow and hold on to your assets.

af.title: Om 'n rekenmeester aan te stel vir klein besigheidsbelasting
af.desc: Om 'n rekenmeester aan te stel vir die indiening van jou klein besigheidsbelasting is goedkoper en hulle sal ook jou belasting betyds indien.
af.html:
Om belasting vir jou klein besigheid in te dien kan tydrowend wees. En terwyl jy dalk 'n deeglike kennis van jou finansiële situasie kry en gereed is om oortyd te werk wanneer dit by belastingseisoen kom, is die kanse dat dit onvolhoubaar is. 

Soms dink jy dalk dat om jou belastingverantwoordelikhede aan 'n geoktrooieerde rekenmeester te gee elke probleem sal oplos, maar jy versuim om hul tarief met jou alledaagse rekenmeester te vergelyk. Om 'n rekenmeester aan te stel sal jou toelaat om verslae te ontvang wanneer dit ook al nodig is teen 'n goedkoper tarief. Hulle sal ook jou belasting betyds indien. 

Deeltydse rekenmeester

Miskien vereis jou begroting of behoeftes slegs gedeeltelike dienste van 'n rekenmeester. Om 'n deeltydse rekenmeester aan te stel beteken laer aanhouerfooie. As jy nie van plan is om hul dienste die hele jaar deur te gebruik nie, dan is uitkontraktering jou beste opsie. Jy mag dalk 'n toegewyde boekhouer nodig hê en slegs 'n beroep doen op jou belastingadviseur teen belastingseisoen. Klein besigheidsbelasting vereis akkurate boekhouding onafhanklik van jou rekenmeester. 

Deeltydse rekenmeesters verstaan dat klein besighede hul belasting moet verminder, en hulle weet wanneer en hoe om regulasies in hul guns toe te pas. Weet jy nie of jy kwalifiseer vir belastingverligting nie? Hulle ken jou situasie en hoe om dit te maksimeer.

Rekenmeesters bied bykomende voordele soos die beplanning van rekeningkundige strategieë en het onvermydelik ervaring om in 'n span te werk.

Wat om te oorweeg wanneer jy 'n rekenmeester vir jou belasting aanstel

 As jy nie vertroud is met of nie verwysings het wanneer jy 'n belastingrekenmeester aanstel nie, hier is 'n paar vrae om te oorweeg wanneer jy jou eerste besigheidsrekenmeester aanstel om hul moontlike vlak van bydrae tot jou besigheid te peil.

Sal hulle jou bystaan buite jou belastingbehoeftes?

Belastingseisoen is wanneer hulle die nodigste is, maar hul rol beïnvloed jou wanneer dit ook by ouditering kom (afhangende van jou PI-telling). Is hulle beskikbaar buite belastingseisoen? As hulle nie 'n vol skedule die hele jaar deur het nie, is hulle ten minste die soort persoon wat proaktief op datum sou bly met die veranderinge in belastingwette?  

Beskikbaarheid: wat is hul skedule?

Kan jy verskeie kere per jaar op hulle staatmaak? 'n Maand? Wanneer jy ook al finansiële advies nodig het? Stel hulle meer belang daarin om hoër-betalende kliënte te help in plaas daarvan om jou klein besigheidsbelasting op 'n konsekwente basis te bestuur? Sulke besonderhede word beter geken voordat jy hulle vir jou rekeningkundige behoeftes betrek.

Vlak van ervaring in verhouding tot jou belastingbehoeftes?

Beskikbaarheid is een ding, maar vorige ervaring met soortgelyke besighede kan beteken om relevante afskrywings en belastingaanspreeklikheid te vind voordat sperdatums verbygaan. Is hulle goed onderleë, maar nie waar jy hul probleemoplossingsvaardighede nodig het nie? Vind 'n rekenmeester met relevante ervaring deur meer as een te onderhoud.

Met die regte rekenmeester wat jou met jou klein besigheidsbelasting help, kan jy sekere voordele verwag soos: 

Belastingnakoming

Soos elke ander onderneming word klein besigheidsbelasting dieselfde behandel. Jy het steeds dieselfde verantwoordelikhede, om nie te praat van dieselfde hulpbronne nie. Om jou jaarlikse belastingstate saam te stel en in te dien word meer doeltreffend gemaak met eFiling. Om rekenmeesters by te hou wat belasting verstaan kan bydra tot jou besigheid se aftrekkings en die werklading vir die rekeningkundige afdeling verminder. 

Weet jy of jou klein of mediumgrootte besigheid kwalifiseer vir omsetbelasting (TOT)? Die verskil in jou jaarlikse wins kan geweldig beïnvloed word as jy beide inkomstebelasting en BTW betaal in teenstelling met die betaling van TOT. Om 'n rekenmeester oor sulke aangeleenthede aan te stel kan jou finansiële stand 'n paar kerwe ophef. Hulle kan ook doeltreffende belastingprosesse vir die volgende werknemer instel. Herstrukturering is nie die maklikste taak nie, maar met 'n intuïtiewe verstand wat onderleë is in klein besigheidsbelasting, sal jy jouself op die regte pad gelei het. 

Terwyl jy jou jaarlikse belastingopgawes aanlyn kan doen, sal die aanstelling van 'n rekenmeester die proses vereenvoudig en jou moeiteloos nakomend hou met regerende belastingregulasies.

Advies in belastingopgawes en aftrekkings

Die nommer een werk van jou rekenmeester is om jou geld te bespaar op jou klein besigheidsbelasting. Hulle weet waar om aansoek te doen vir vrystellings, wat om te registreer as jaarlikse belastingaftrekkings, en jou algehele kontantvloei te verhoog as gevolg van die indiening van akkurate belastingopgawes.

Om te weet watter aftrekkings gemaak moet word kan oorweldigend wees, maar om 'n rekenmeester te hê om jou te help om besluite te neem (soos die keuse tussen 'n gestandaardiseerde aftrekking of 'n gespesifiseerde aftrekking) is van onskatbare waarde. As ervare veterane in belastingaftrekkings kan hulle help om jou klein besigheidsbelasting aansienlik te verminder.

Hul werk word makliker gemaak met goed-gedokumenteerde finansiële rekords wat deur 'n boekhouer bestuur word. Die twee sou dikwels hand aan hand werk. 

Sekere professionele rekenmeesters soos geoktrooieerde rekenmeesters of ingeskrewe agente kan jou ook adviseer oor jou klein besigheidsbelasting. Beide is opgelei in belastingverligtings en alle aangeleenthede wat met belasting verband hou soos genoem. Terwyl jy op jou rekenmeester sou staatmaak vir ander klein besigheidsbelastingdienste, sou jy meer baat vind by in-diepte konsultasie van gespesialiseerde rekenmeesters.  Met 'n toename in winste, terugbetalings en vrystellings is jy in staat om kontantvloei te verhoog en aan jou bates vas te hou.

posts.title: The essential accountant services a business needs in South Africa.
posts.excerpt: Accountant services form the basis of any business’s means to stay afloat. From accountants managing cash flow to bookkeepers processing payroll, if you want to grow your business you cannot do without their services.  Whether your accountant services are outsourced to a firm or your business has these services provided from within, every business needs ... Read more
posts.desc: Accountant services are essential for any business to stay afloat; From accountants managing cash flow to bookkeepers processing payroll.
posts.html:
Accountant services form the basis of any business’s means to stay afloat. From accountants managing cash flow to bookkeepers processing payroll, if you want to grow your business you cannot do without their services. 

Whether your accountant services are outsourced to a firm or your business has these services provided from within, every business needs them.

In this blog we’ll discuss the six accountant services every South African business needs:

  1. Bookkeeping & managing inventory
  2. Taxation services
  3. Financial statements for budgeting and strategy 
  4. Payroll services
  5. Financial tracking
  6. Financial advising 

Bookkeeping & managing inventory

The first step to grow your business, from an SME to your ideal enterprise or otherwise, is to know your day-to-day transactions. This is recorded in your general ledger. It is one of the accountant services you will have to keep from day one. If you plan on growing your business in the future, you’re 100 percent likely to undergo an audit or an independent review. Having your books in order with an accurate bookkeeper will keep you at ease.

If your profits come from a product, stock management should be kept tight. Having untrustworthy employees that take your products one by one may be difficult to track. You don’t want to be running a business at a loss. Without cross-checking your records, and possibly the security cameras, such occurrences may go unnoticed. 

Taxation services

The goal is to have your financial documentation up to date so you don’t worry about missing the tax deadline come tax season. Once again, never underestimate the skills of a dedicated bookkeeper; they can save you much time come tax season. Late penalties are costly too! 

One thing a bookkeeper won’t be able to help you with, however, that an accountant will, is filing your taxes. Whether it be strategies, giving advice, or filing returns, having the right mind behind it can keep you compliant and free from worry.

Financial statements for budgeting and strategy

Budgeting is one service that potentially determines a business’s future and a task where accountants may thrive. For accountants doing your business’s strategic financial planning, having organised statements allows them to make better predictions. This, obviously, clears up the guesswork and enables higher chances of achieving your annual targets. Weighing in heavily amongst accountant services, this task, along with accurate bookkeeping, forms the building blocks of all business financing.

Keeping your statements updated at all times makes auditing run faster. Providing documentation promptly is always in your favour.

Investors will need an analysis of a company’s finances, and the chances of increasing the sum of their initial investment could depend on this. With a gain in trust of audits due to the expectation gap being addressed, you can compound the public’s trust. Such means would include having your records straight (not practicing fraud) and consulting before the audit process. We’ll elaborate on financial consulting in a moment.

Payroll services

One of the overlooked, yet highly beneficial, accountant services for most small businesses is payroll services. It is among the first financial services considered when it comes to outsourcing. An accountant that manages your employees’ salaries, sets up their accounts, maintains a level of security, and gets them paid before the month ends are essential aspects that can significantly lighten the load of business owners.  

Financial tracking

Not following up on your accounts receivable could be the reason a profitable business lacks cash flow and is unable to pay for daily services or salaries. The time it takes to follow up and eventually secure accounts receivable could be the time you use to maintain a profitable business. Outsourcing such accountant services will take away the nagging that may accompany your demand for payment. Outsourced services will ask in a professional yet urgent manner. 

The flipside will be managing your accounts payable. With a set budget in place after strategising with your accountant, this task will be carried out seamlessly.

Financial advising

Though it may not be as essential a financial service at the start, it may later prove to be a valuable asset. Calling in on chartered accountant services to consult you on structuring your business’s finances will fast-track your sales and reporting. If your accountant takes the time to understand your business and learn about your targets at the early stages, you may find unexplored insights sooner when reviewing their advice. This will set you up for leaps and bounds in your business’s growth.

If you’re far into your entrepreneur journey, financial consulting could still help with restructuring your finances to a more streamlined set-up. Maybe your business is being audited for the first time and you want to tackle the task with precision, avoiding stress and hours away from your tasks.

Setting the bar high when it comes to audits means more financial accountability and that leads to an increase in numbers on the profit side.

af.title: Die noodsaaklike rekenmeesterdienste wat 'n besigheid in Suid-Afrika benodig.
af.desc: Rekenmeesterdienste is noodsaaklik vir enige besigheid om kop bo water te hou; Van rekenmeesters wat kontantvloei bestuur tot boekhouers wat loonstaat verwerk.
af.html:
Rekenmeesterdienste vorm die basis van enige besigheid se vermoë om kop bo water te hou. Van rekenmeesters wat kontantvloei bestuur tot boekhouers wat loonstaat verwerk, as jy jou besigheid wil laat groei kan jy nie sonder hul dienste klaarkom nie. 

Of jou rekenmeesterdienste aan 'n firma uitgekontrakteer word of jou besigheid hierdie dienste van binne verskaf word, elke besigheid het hulle nodig.

In hierdie blog sal ons die ses rekenmeesterdienste bespreek wat elke Suid-Afrikaanse besigheid benodig:

  1. Boekhouding en voorraadbestuur
  2. Belastingdienste
  3. Finansiële state vir begroting en strategie 
  4. Loonstaatdienste
  5. Finansiële nasporing
  6. Finansiële advisering 

Boekhouding en voorraadbestuur

Die eerste stap om jou besigheid te laat groei, van 'n KMO tot jou ideale onderneming of andersins, is om jou daaglikse transaksies te ken. Dit word in jou grootboek aangeteken. Dit is een van die rekenmeesterdienste wat jy van dag een af sal moet byhou. As jy van plan is om jou besigheid in die toekoms te laat groei, is jy 100 persent waarskynlik om 'n oudit of 'n onafhanklike hersiening te ondergaan. Om jou boeke in orde te hê met 'n akkurate boekhouer sal jou gerus hou.

As jou winste van 'n produk kom, moet voorraadbestuur stip gehou word. Om onbetroubare werknemers te hê wat jou produkte een vir een neem mag moeilik wees om na te spoor. Jy wil nie 'n besigheid teen 'n verlies bedryf nie. Sonder om jou rekords, en moontlik die sekuriteitskameras, te kruiskontroleer, mag sulke voorvalle ongemerk verbygaan. 

Belastingdienste

Die doel is om jou finansiële dokumentasie op datum te hê sodat jy nie hoef te bekommer oor die mis van die belastingsperdatum teen belastingseisoen nie. Weer eens, moet nooit die vaardighede van 'n toegewyde boekhouer onderskat nie; hulle kan jou baie tyd bespaar teen belastingseisoen. Laat boetes is ook duur! 

Een ding waarmee 'n boekhouer jou egter nie sal kan help nie, wat 'n rekenmeester sal, is om jou belasting in te dien. Of dit nou strategieë, die gee van advies, of die indiening van opgawes is, om die regte verstand daaragter te hê kan jou nakomend en vry van bekommernis hou.

Finansiële state vir begroting en strategie

Begroting is een diens wat potensieel 'n besigheid se toekoms bepaal en 'n taak waar rekenmeesters mag floreer. Vir rekenmeesters wat jou besigheid se strategiese finansiële beplanning doen, laat georganiseerde state hulle toe om beter voorspellings te maak. Dit, vanselfsprekend, maak die raaiwerk skoon en stel hoër kanse in staat om jou jaarlikse teikens te bereik. Hierdie taak, wat swaar weeg onder rekenmeesterdienste, vorm saam met akkurate boekhouding die boustene van alle besigheidsfinansiering.

Om jou state te alle tye op datum te hou maak ouditering vinniger. Om dokumentasie stiptelik te verskaf is altyd in jou guns.

Beleggers sal 'n ontleding van 'n maatskappy se finansies nodig hê, en die kanse om die som van hul aanvanklike belegging te verhoog kan hiervan afhang. Met 'n toename in vertroue van oudits as gevolg van die verwagtingsgaping wat aangespreek word, kan jy die publiek se vertroue saamstel. Sulke middele sou insluit om jou rekords reg te hê (nie bedrog te pleeg nie) en te konsulteer voor die ouditproses. Ons sal binne 'n oomblik oor finansiële konsultasie uitbrei.

Loonstaatdienste

Een van die oor die hoof gesiene, maar hoogs voordelige, rekenmeesterdienste vir die meeste klein besighede is loonstaatdienste. Dit is onder die eerste finansiële dienste wat oorweeg word wanneer dit by uitkontraktering kom. 'n Rekenmeester wat jou werknemers se salarisse bestuur, hul rekeninge opstel, 'n vlak van sekuriteit handhaaf, en hulle laat betaal voor die maand eindig is noodsaaklike aspekte wat die las van besigheidseienaars aansienlik kan verlig.  

Finansiële nasporing

Om nie jou debiteure op te volg nie kan die rede wees dat 'n winsgewende besigheid kontantvloei kortkom en nie in staat is om vir daaglikse dienste of salarisse te betaal nie. Die tyd wat dit neem om op te volg en uiteindelik debiteure te bekom kan die tyd wees wat jy gebruik om 'n winsgewende besigheid te handhaaf. Om sulke rekenmeesterdienste uit te kontrakteer sal die geneul wegneem wat jou eis om betaling mag vergesel. Uitgekontrakteerde dienste sal op 'n professionele maar dringende manier vra. 

Die keersy sal die bestuur van jou krediteure wees. Met 'n vasgestelde begroting in plek nadat jy met jou rekenmeester gestrategiseer het, sal hierdie taak naatloos uitgevoer word.

Finansiële advisering

Alhoewel dit dalk nie so 'n noodsaaklike finansiële diens aan die begin is nie, mag dit later 'n waardevolle bate blyk te wees. Om 'n beroep te doen op geoktrooieerde rekenmeester-dienste om jou te raadpleeg oor die strukturering van jou besigheid se finansies sal jou verkope en verslagdoening versnel. As jou rekenmeester die tyd neem om jou besigheid te verstaan en oor jou teikens in die vroeë stadiums te leer, mag jy onverkende insigte vroeër vind wanneer jy hul advies hersien. Dit sal jou opstel vir groot vooruitgang in jou besigheid se groei.

As jy ver in jou entrepreneursreis is, kan finansiële konsultasie steeds help met die herstrukturering van jou finansies na 'n meer vaartbelynde opstelling. Miskien word jou besigheid vir die eerste keer geoudit en wil jy die taak met presisie aanpak, om stres en ure weg van jou take te vermy.

Om die maatstaf hoog te stel wanneer dit by oudits kom beteken meer finansiële aanspreeklikheid en dit lei tot 'n toename in syfers aan die winskant.

posts.title: Accountants vs bookkeepers: what’s the difference?
posts.excerpt: The role of accountants vs bookkeepers differs in many aspects, and if you’re confused about their roles, this post will help to clear up any misunderstandings. In the simplest of terms, the job of an accountant cannot be done by a bookkeeper, yet accountants can do your bookkeeping. While you might think of giving the ... Read more
posts.desc: Learn more about the difference between accountants vs bookkeepers. Both need an eye for detail as small mistakes could lead to major disruptions
posts.html:
The role of accountants vs bookkeepers differs in many aspects, and if you’re confused about their roles, this post will help to clear up any misunderstandings. In the simplest of terms, the job of an accountant cannot be done by a bookkeeper, yet accountants can do your bookkeeping.

While you might think of giving the bookkeeping role to an accountant, you’ll realise that the large volume of records to be kept will take away from an accountant’s essential tasks.

Primary roles: Accountants vs bookkeepers

Accountants and bookkeepers work closely together, with bookkeepers providing the necessary financial data that an accountant will assess. Both utilise mathematics and number-crunching, and they need an eye for detail as small mistakes could lead to major disruptions down the line.

Activities & skills

Accountants work more on forecasting financial data. They will make financial decisions and advise management on ways to increase profit margins. They rely heavily on the accuracy of books prepared by bookkeepers and analyse the financial data. 

They also look into the processes behind the record-keeping to implement best practices and keep business compliant and out of risk. Auditing falls into their role and could be called upon when applying for a bank loan.

Bookkeepers track a business’s finances. The bookkeeper works in the general ledger and thus knows of every transaction that occurs in the company. Sub-tasks include different ways of recording entries and implementing best practices for single or double entries.

Their involvement is crucial on a day-to-day basis, which would be the main difference between accountants’ vs bookkeepers’ time in the office. With respect to their accuracy in filing, they too help keep businesses compliant. Bookkeepers also process payroll. Using their knowledge of the payments that a business makes, they may deal with reimbursements if ever there is such a case.

You may now understand why giving your bookkeeping to an accountant will fill up their working hours, not to mention the increased cost of hiring the expert.

Documents prepared

Accountants handle documentation on a business’s corporate tax returns and any financial advice behind decisions. They too can prepare cash-flow or balance sheet statements, but often work on strategies related to business goals. 

Bookkeepers verify documents and compile budget reports. If a company has software to keep their books, it is still necessary to have someone check up on this system. Besides precision and attention to detail, bookkeepers need to be tech-savvy or at the very least adaptable and open to learning.

Tax roles

The complexity of accountant’s vs bookkeeper’s roles in tax varies significantly, with an accountant’s tasks being more complex.

Bookkeepers are not authorised to perform in-depth services with regard to taxation; however, they may perform calculations on your business’s tax deductions. They have the task of keeping invoices compliant with the VAT act and thereby preparing all the documentation for accountants come tax season. 

Strategizing, filing, and planning for tax season are the roles of your accountant. Chartered accountants are allowed to sign off on your tax returns if all procedures are met.   

Certifications, Salaries & the journey ahead

If you’re one of the few people who thrive on number crunching and interpretation, then you’ve likely got the chance to advance rapidly through your career. For many accountants, their years of study are long but pay handsomely in the end.

Education

While it is possible to become a self-taught bookkeeper, most would have to supplement courses and almost always become certified before entering their line of work.

Accountants have to attain a degree, in either accounting business or finance, and pass standardised exams while oftentimes needing to undergo continual training to remain certified accounting practitioners. Many accounting career paths, which we will touch on in a moment, require specialised exams and a master’s degree. 

Salary

It is a known fact that accountants earn more than bookkeepers, yet starting salaries vary. In the long term, accountants also tend to earn more and it is worthwhile for bookkeepers to study further to attain the qualification. It is not unheard of for bookkeepers to work freelance and earn wages, but more often than not they are hired for the long term. 

Career paths

Bookkeeping can be the first step in your accounting journey, but most accountants go through a period of formal education and hours of practicing. Accountants earn more with their certifications and are able to advance into spaces of tax specialization or auditing. Future careers could include chartered accounting, becoming an enrolled agent, and many more.  

Summary: Key differences

The differences in accountant’s and bookkeeper’s education, initial and long-term earnings, tax roles, and business contributions are considerable. While bookkeeper’s salaries are lower, their overall skillset can still help small businesses to remain compliant while keeping records straight for an external audit. 

Accountants can take up managing roles, given their formative background, and can bring a business more profit and help manage the cash flow. 

While bookkeepers handle journals and the general ledger, accountants make use of this data to create financial projections that help grow small businesses.

af.title: Rekenmeesters versus boekhouers: wat is die verskil?
af.desc: Leer meer oor die verskil tussen rekenmeesters versus boekhouers. Beide het ’n oog vir detail nodig aangesien klein foute groot ontwrigtings kan veroorsaak.
af.html:
Die rol van rekenmeesters versus boekhouers verskil in baie opsigte, en as jy verward is oor hul rolle, sal hierdie pos help om enige misverstande uit die weg te ruim. In die eenvoudigste terme kan die werk van ’n rekenmeester nie deur ’n boekhouer gedoen word nie, tog kan rekenmeesters jou boekhouding doen.

Terwyl jy dalk daaraan dink om die boekhoudingsrol aan ’n rekenmeester te gee, sal jy besef dat die groot volume rekords wat gehou moet word, sal wegneem van ’n rekenmeester se noodsaaklike take.

Primere rolle: Rekenmeesters versus boekhouers

Rekenmeesters en boekhouers werk nou saam, met boekhouers wat die nodige finansiele data verskaf wat ’n rekenmeester sal assesseer. Beide gebruik wiskunde en syferverwerking, en hulle het ’n oog vir detail nodig aangesien klein foute later groot ontwrigtings kan veroorsaak.

Aktiwiteite en vaardighede

Rekenmeesters werk meer aan die voorspelling van finansiele data. Hulle sal finansiele besluite neem en bestuur adviseer oor maniere om winsmarges te verhoog. Hulle maak swaar staat op die akkuraatheid van boeke wat deur boekhouers voorberei word en ontleed die finansiele data. 

Hulle kyk ook na die prosesse agter die rekordhouding om beste praktyke te implementeer en die besigheid voldoende en uit risiko te hou. Ouditering val binne hul rol en kan ingeroep word wanneer aansoek gedoen word vir ’n banklening.

Boekhouers spoor ’n besigheid se finansies na. Die boekhouer werk in die grootboek en weet dus van elke transaksie wat in die maatskappy plaasvind. Subtake sluit verskillende maniere in om inskrywings aan te teken en beste praktyke te implementeer vir enkel- of dubbelinskrywings.

Hul betrokkenheid is op ’n dag-tot-dag basis van kardinale belang, wat die hoofverskil sou wees tussen rekenmeesters versus boekhouers se tyd op kantoor. Met betrekking tot hul akkuraatheid in indiening, help hulle ook om besighede voldoende te hou. Boekhouers verwerk ook betaalstate. Deur hul kennis van die betalings wat ’n besigheid maak te gebruik, kan hulle met terugbetalings handel as daar ooit so ’n geval is.

Jy mag nou verstaan waarom om jou boekhouding aan ’n rekenmeester te gee hul werksure sal opvul, om nie te praat van die verhoogde koste om die deskundige aan te stel nie.

Dokumente voorberei

Rekenmeesters hanteer dokumentasie oor ’n besigheid se korporatiewe belastingopgawes en enige finansiele advies agter besluite. Hulle kan ook kontantvloei- of balansstaatstate voorberei, maar werk dikwels aan strategieë wat met besigheidsdoelwitte verband hou. 

Boekhouers verifieer dokumente en stel begrotingsverslae saam. As ’n maatskappy sagteware het om hul boeke te hou, is dit steeds nodig om iemand te hê wat hierdie stelsel nagaan. Behalwe presisie en aandag aan detail, moet boekhouers tegnologies vaardig wees of ten minste aanpasbaar en oop vir leer.

Belastingrolle

Die kompleksiteit van rekenmeesters versus boekhouers se rolle in belasting verskil aansienlik, met ’n rekenmeester se take wat meer kompleks is.

Boekhouers is nie gemagtig om diepgaande dienste met betrekking tot belasting uit te voer nie; hulle mag egter berekenings op jou besigheid se belastingaftrekkings doen. Hulle het die taak om fakture voldoende aan die BTW-wet te hou en daardeur al die dokumentasie vir rekenmeesters voor te berei teen belastingseisoen.

Strategisering, indiening, en beplanning vir belastingseisoen is die rolle van jou rekenmeester. Geoktrooieerde rekenmeesters word toegelaat om jou belastingopgawes goed te keur as alle prosedures nagekom word.   

Sertifisering, salarisse en die reis vorentoe

As jy een van die min mense is wat floreer op syferverwerking en interpretasie, dan het jy waarskynlik die kans om vinnig deur jou loopbaan te vorder. Vir baie rekenmeesters is hul jare van studie lank, maar betaal mooi op die ou end.

Opvoeding

Alhoewel dit moontlik is om ’n selfopgeleide boekhouer te word, sal die meeste kursusse moet aanvul en byna altyd gesertifiseer word voordat hulle hul beroep betree.

Rekenmeesters moet ’n graad behaal, in óf rekeningkunde, besigheid óf finansies, en gestandaardiseerde eksamens slaag terwyl hulle dikwels deurlopende opleiding moet ondergaan om gesertifiseerde rekeningkundige praktisyns te bly. Baie rekeningkundige loopbaanpaaie, waaroor ons binnekort sal aanraak, vereis gespesialiseerde eksamens en ’n meestersgraad. 

Salaris

Dit is ’n bekende feit dat rekenmeesters meer verdien as boekhouers, tog wissel aanvangssalarisse. Op die lang termyn is rekenmeesters ook geneig om meer te verdien en dit is die moeite werd vir boekhouers om verder te studeer om die kwalifikasie te behaal. Dit is nie ongehoord vir boekhouers om vryskut te werk en lone te verdien nie, maar meer dikwels as nie word hulle vir die lang termyn aangestel.

Loopbaanpaaie

Boekhouding kan die eerste stap in jou rekeningkundige reis wees, maar die meeste rekenmeesters gaan deur ’n tydperk van formele opvoeding en ure van oefening. Rekenmeesters verdien meer met hul sertifiserings en is in staat om te vorder na ruimtes van belastingspesialisasie of ouditering. Toekomstige loopbane kan geoktrooieerde rekeningkunde, om ’n geregistreerde agent te word, en vele meer insluit.  

Opsomming: Sleutelverskille

Die verskille in rekenmeesters en boekhouers se opvoeding, aanvanklike en langtermyn-verdienste, belastingrolle en besigheidsbydraes is aansienlik. Terwyl boekhouers se salarisse laer is, kan hul algehele vaardigheidstel steeds klein besighede help om voldoende te bly terwyl rekords reguit gehou word vir ’n eksterne oudit. 

Rekenmeesters kan bestuursrolle opneem, gegewe hul vormende agtergrond, en kan ’n besigheid meer wins inbring en help om die kontantvloei te bestuur. 

Terwyl boekhouers joernale en die grootboek hanteer, maak rekenmeesters gebruik van hierdie data om finansiele projeksies te skep wat help om klein besighede te laat groei.

posts.title: The difference between independent review and audit review
posts.excerpt: Staying in compliance with the amended Companies Act No. 71 of 2008 requires businesses and industrial corporations to be reviewed. It would entail undergoing either an independent review or an audit review. Before this act, businesses had to have a qualified auditor review their financial statements annually. A review provides stakeholders, investors, and banks with ... Read more
posts.desc: In order to determine whether your company must undergo an audit or an IR, one must know their Public Interest Score (PIS). Learn more
posts.html:
Staying in compliance with the amended Companies Act No. 71 of 2008 requires businesses and industrial corporations to be reviewed. It would entail undergoing either an independent review or an audit review. Before this act, businesses had to have a qualified auditor review their financial statements annually. A review provides stakeholders, investors, and banks with assurance. But what’s the difference between these two reviews? And how do I know which is for me? We have the answers!

Independent review (IR) or Auditing: Which is for me? 

In order to determine whether your company must undergo an audit or an IR, one must know their Public Interest Score (PIS). A PIS calculation is required by the Companies and Intellectual Property Commission (CIPC) at the end of every financial year.

Your PI score determines the degree of regulation required for transparency. It is the determining factor when deciding between either review.

 Audits, having higher authority over an IR, apply to companies with a PIS of 350 or above while obtaining an IR will be enough for companies ranking below. If a company is not owner-managed, which is the case for businesses owned by trusts, they would require an independent review.

Scope of reviews: how it affects assurance

With audits having a bigger scope of work, it also provides a “reasonable assurance” while IRs provide “limited assurance” from material misstatements. A study conducted in 2002 by IAASB showed the level of assurance for audits to be between 55 to 98 percent, while IRs were between 10 to 88 percent.

 Both entail inquiry and analytical procedures. This means accountants evaluate financial and non-financial documentation with trends & ratios. Auditors and independent reviewers conduct meetings with board members of a company regarding reporting, documentation, and so forth. This allows them to gain an understanding of the business.

Independent reviews require only a substantive approach, but audits can perform a combined approach if necessary. Other approaches could include a risk-based procedure-based approach. Their primary concern would be to check for irregularities, and secondly for instances of fraud.

Based on a risk assessment, verified financial documentation is sampled with a selection of transactions and other non-financial records. This allows cross-referencing with external factors at certain periods.

The audits must adhere to strict auditing standards, which is an assurance engagement within the entire process. They are more in-depth and detailed in comparison to an IR.

And lastly, the reporting.

The difference in reporting style

To finalise a review, auditors must produce a statement. For an IR, accountants report their opinion of the company in negative assertation. It’s the opposite with an audit; they conclude their report with a positive assertation. 

Both would state any reportable irregularity in the instance of unlawful conduct which amounts to theft. The report would have to detail the irregularity in their respective assertation and mention any measures taken to rectify the misstatements. 

Qualifications needed by auditors

A less obvious fact, before going on, yet an important factor to note when doing a review: the independent reviewer cannot be involved in compiling the documentation.

 Audits need a strictly professional judgement and are conducted by members of the Independent Regulatory Board for Auditors (IRBA), an accredited professional body. IRs can be conducted by the same members or an accounting officer.

Cost & time considerations

Audit reviews are the more costly of the two reviews, because of the factors listed above. With a larger scope of tasks and a need for more proficient accounting judgment, the costs accumulate. Audits generally take more time, while it could differ since task completion is relatively indefinite for either review. Costs also affect the level of assurance with the factor of how thorough a search, at a set price, a client expects.

Audit reviews have a bigger scope providing a more detailed analysis of documentation in their statements. They require costly professionals to carry out the strict scope, and thus provide a “reasonable assurance” for future investors and stakeholders.

While an IR is also good for financial intervening measures, it is also required by the Companies Act if your company is not liable for an audit or is owner-managed. The scope of tasks is less than an audit and they report in a negative assertation, meeting the applicable financial reporting framework.

af.title: Die verskil tussen onafhanklike hersiening en oudithersiening
af.desc: Om te bepaal of jou maatskappy 'n oudit of 'n IR moet ondergaan, moet 'n mens jou Openbare Belangtelling (PIS) ken. Leer meer
af.html:
Om in nakoming te bly met die gewysigde Maatskappywet No. 71 van 2008 vereis dat besighede en industriële korporasies hersien word. Dit sou behels om óf 'n onafhanklike hersiening óf 'n oudithersiening te ondergaan. Voor hierdie wet moes besighede 'n gekwalifiseerde ouditeur hê om hul finansiële state jaarliks te hersien. 'n Hersiening verskaf versekering aan belanghebbendes, beleggers en banke. Maar wat is die verskil tussen hierdie twee hersienings? En hoe weet ek watter een vir my is? Ons het die antwoorde!

Onafhanklike hersiening (IR) of Ouditering: Watter een is vir my? 

Om te bepaal of jou maatskappy 'n oudit of 'n IR moet ondergaan, moet 'n mens jou Openbare Belangtelling (PIS) ken. 'n PIS-berekening word vereis deur die Companies and Intellectual Property Commission (CIPC) aan die einde van elke finansiële jaar.

Jou PI-telling bepaal die graad van regulering wat vir deursigtigheid vereis word. Dit is die deurslaggewende faktor wanneer daar tussen enige hersiening besluit word.

 Oudits, met hoër gesag oor 'n IR, geld vir maatskappye met 'n PIS van 350 of hoër terwyl die verkryging van 'n IR genoeg sal wees vir maatskappye wat daaronder rangeer. As 'n maatskappy nie eienaar-bestuur is nie, wat die geval is vir besighede wat deur trusts besit word, sou hulle 'n onafhanklike hersiening vereis.

Omvang van hersienings: hoe dit versekering beïnvloed

Met oudits wat 'n groter omvang van werk het, verskaf dit ook 'n “redelike versekering” terwyl IR's “beperkte versekering” teen wesenlike wanvoorstellings verskaf. 'n Studie wat in 2002 deur IAASB gedoen is het getoon dat die vlak van versekering vir oudits tussen 55 en 98 persent is, terwyl IR's tussen 10 en 88 persent was.

 Beide behels ondersoek- en analitiese prosedures. Dit beteken rekenmeesters evalueer finansiële en nie-finansiële dokumentasie met tendense en verhoudings. Ouditeure en onafhanklike hersieners hou vergaderings met direksielede van 'n maatskappy aangaande verslagdoening, dokumentasie, ensovoorts. Dit laat hulle toe om 'n begrip van die besigheid te kry.

Onafhanklike hersienings vereis slegs 'n substantiewe benadering, maar oudits kan 'n gekombineerde benadering uitvoer indien nodig. Ander benaderings kan 'n risikogebaseerde prosedure-gebaseerde benadering insluit. Hul primêre bekommernis sou wees om vir onreëlmatighede te kontroleer, en tweedens vir gevalle van bedrog.

Gebaseer op 'n risiko-assessering word geverifieerde finansiële dokumentasie gemonster met 'n seleksie van transaksies en ander nie-finansiële rekords. Dit laat kruisverwysing met eksterne faktore by sekere periodes toe.

Die oudits moet by streng ouditstandaarde hou, wat 'n versekeringsverbintenis binne die hele proses is. Hulle is meer in-diepte en gedetailleerd in vergelyking met 'n IR.

En laastens, die verslagdoening.

Die verskil in verslagdoeningstyl

Om 'n hersiening te finaliseer, moet ouditeure 'n verklaring produseer. Vir 'n IR rapporteer rekenmeesters hul opinie van die maatskappy in negatiewe bewering. Dit is die teenoorgestelde met 'n oudit; hulle sluit hul verslag af met 'n positiewe bewering. 

Beide sou enige rapporteerbare onreëlmatigheid verklaar in die geval van onwettige optrede wat op diefstal neerkom. Die verslag sou die onreëlmatigheid in hul onderskeie bewering moet uiteensit en enige maatreëls wat geneem is om die wanvoorstellings reg te stel, moet noem. 

Kwalifikasies wat deur ouditeure benodig word

'n Minder voor die hand liggende feit, voordat ons voortgaan, maar 'n belangrike faktor om op te let wanneer 'n hersiening gedoen word: die onafhanklike hersiener kan nie betrokke wees by die samestelling van die dokumentasie nie.

 Oudits benodig 'n streng professionele oordeel en word uitgevoer deur lede van die Independent Regulatory Board for Auditors (IRBA), 'n geakkrediteerde professionele liggaam. IR's kan deur dieselfde lede of 'n rekeningkundige beampte uitgevoer word.

Koste- en tydoorwegings

Oudithersienings is die duurste van die twee hersienings, weens die faktore hierbo gelys. Met 'n groter omvang van take en 'n behoefte aan meer bekwame rekeningkundige oordeel, akkumuleer die koste. Oudits neem oor die algemeen meer tyd, terwyl dit kan verskil aangesien taakvoltooiing relatief onbepaald is vir enige hersiening. Koste beïnvloed ook die vlak van versekering met die faktor van hoe deeglik 'n soektog, teen 'n vasgestelde prys, 'n kliënt verwag.

Oudithersienings het 'n groter omvang wat 'n meer gedetailleerde ontleding van dokumentasie in hul state verskaf. Hulle vereis duur professionele persone om die streng omvang uit te voer, en verskaf dus 'n “redelike versekering” vir toekomstige beleggers en belanghebbendes.

Terwyl 'n IR ook goed is vir finansiële ingrypende maatreëls, word dit ook deur die Maatskappywet vereis as jou maatskappy nie aanspreeklik is vir 'n oudit nie of eienaar-bestuur is. Die omvang van take is minder as 'n oudit en hulle rapporteer in 'n negatiewe bewering, wat aan die toepaslike finansiële verslagdoeningsraamwerk voldoen.

posts.title: 5 reasons why companies outsource payroll in South Africa
posts.excerpt: The process of payroll includes not only the due payments to employees but also other work benefits such as with-held taxes and bonuses. Companies might think to outsource payroll whence they realise that keeping track of payroll records becomes a hefty task. An insight in the Emerald journals showed there was an efficiency gain with additional tasks ... Read more
posts.desc: Business owners outsource payroll to accounting firms as it comes with many benefits compared to having the process in-house. Learn more.
posts.html:
The process of payroll includes not only the due payments to employees but also other work benefits such as with-held taxes and bonuses. Companies might think to outsource payroll whence they realise that keeping track of payroll records becomes a hefty task. An insight in the Emerald journals showed there was an efficiency gain with additional tasks of payroll being outsourced.

Why outsource payroll services?

This is the question you may be asking yourself: In-house payroll departments have managed the job so far. Besides gaining in the efficiency of your tasks, which allows you to work on important tasks surrounding growth factors in your business, what are the other benefits that you could expect if you outsource your payroll services to an accounting firm? 

Here are the 5 reasons to outsource payroll:

Less expense

Every company wants to increase its profit margins, and a factor of that equation includes decreasing expenses. 

Reduce staffing & training costs

The choice to outsource payroll comes with the benefit of the service without the salary. There is no need to pay new employees to do a task, whilst later requiring means to increase their salary as compensation.

Accurate & reliable service

The consequence of mismanaged and untimely paid dues leads to a decrease in overall productivity amongst employees. Their attitudes and motivations potentially stand the risk of worsening. 

Excludes guesswork

Companies that outsource payroll have ease of mind. Their employees are paid promptly and everything looks seamless. That comes from experience and time invested in companies by outsourced accounting firms. They have had to manage many payrolls prior to yours, and have had the insight to tweak the service to your company, no matter the size. 

Their accountants are more so compliant, having to manage more accounts and know the effects of accurate reporting later down the line.

Access to resources

Every company wants to remain compliant, and having to keep in line with government regulations could often thin out cash flow and resources. There is no need for spending funds on purchasing & maintaining accounting software when you choose to outsource payroll.

Accounting software helps with tax-compliant invoices

By design, the use of accounting software enables a greater degree of compliance. Automated technology has the potential to convert quotes to invoices.

For companies with large sales volumes and overseas clients, the benefit of outsourcing payroll to firms familiar with laws concerning electronic invoicing and the latest software is obvious. Non-compliance penalties for invalid invoices are a burden none wants during audits. Such software voids this problem, whilst solving scheduling conflicts.

Saves your time

Productivity beats busyness, and if you’re spending time on supplementary tasks then your time and money are greatly underutilised. Every company should have the flexibility to put their efforts in the right place and could afford to when they outsource payroll services.

No training! Less admin! 

Besides training costs, the time taken from upskilling employees takes away from work hours and later takes up time in their schedule. Outsourced accounting firms bring payroll services along with automated software, and thus a faster billing process overall. No need to wait on payment administration. Any reasons to not outsource payroll services should vanish.

Secure & confidential

When setting up your payroll, the concern of security adds to the hefty process. Once-off security measures could lead to an overall long-term undermining of the payroll process, even if you’ve managed to automate the system. 

Less risk

 If your company chooses to outsource payroll to an accounting firm, they also mitigate risks. Accounting firms are aware of this responsibility and avoid any chance of faltering payments. Since the payments and benefactors belong to an external company, they take care to validate details at each part of the process. Each salary paid is treated with measures to ensure there are no mix-ups.   

Whilst there are numerous reasons to outsource payroll, the fact remains that such an essential service should be handled by professional accountants. If your company needs time, access to accounting software or intends to save on costs, the first task to be considered should be payroll service. Clearing this task from your accountant’s hands alleviates stress and gives you a greater degree of compliance with regard to eFiling.

af.title: 5 redes waarom maatskappye loonadministrasie in Suid-Afrika uitkontrakteer
af.desc: Besigheidseienaars kontrakteer loonadministrasie aan rekenmeesterfirmas uit weens die vele voordele bo interne hantering. Lees meer.
af.html:
Die proses van loonadministrasie sluit nie net die verskuldigde betalings aan werknemers in nie, maar ook ander werkvoordele soos teruggehoue belasting en bonusse. Maatskappye dink dalk daaraan om loonadministrasie uit te kontrakteer wanneer hulle besef dat dit ’n swaar taak word om loonrekords by te hou. ’n Insig in die Emerald-vaktydskrifte het getoon dat daar ’n doeltreffendheidswins was wanneer bykomende loonadministrasietake uitgekontrakteer word.

Waarom loonadministrasiedienste uitkontrakteer?

Dit is die vraag wat jy dalk vir jouself vra: Interne loonadministrasie-afdelings het die werk tot dusver behartig. Behalwe vir die wins in die doeltreffendheid van jou take, wat jou toelaat om aan belangrike take rondom groeifaktore in jou besigheid te werk, watter ander voordele kan jy verwag indien jy jou loonadministrasiedienste aan ’n rekenmeesterfirma uitkontrakteer? 

Hier is die 5 redes om loonadministrasie uit te kontrakteer:

Minder uitgawes

Elke maatskappy wil sy winsmarges verhoog, en deel van daardie vergelyking is om uitgawes te verminder. 

Verminder personeel- & opleidingskoste

Die keuse om loonadministrasie uit te kontrakteer bring die voordeel van die diens sonder die salaris. Daar is geen nodigheid om nuwe werknemers te betaal om ’n taak te doen nie, terwyl jy later middele moet vind om hul salaris as vergoeding te verhoog.

Akkurate & betroubare diens

Die gevolg van wanbestuurde en ontydig betaalde verskuldighede lei tot ’n afname in algehele produktiwiteit onder werknemers. Hul houdings en motiverings staan moontlik die risiko om te versleg. 

Sluit raaiwerk uit

Maatskappye wat loonadministrasie uitkontrakteer het gemoedsrus. Hul werknemers word stiptelik betaal en alles lyk naatloos. Dit kom van ervaring en tyd wat deur uitgekontrakteerde rekenmeesterfirmas in maatskappye belê is. Hulle moes baie loonadministrasies voor joune bestuur, en het die insig gehad om die diens by jou maatskappy aan te pas, ongeag die grootte. 

Hul rekenmeesters is des te meer voldoenend, aangesien hulle meer rekeninge moet bestuur en die gevolge van akkurate verslagdoening verder aan ken.

Toegang tot hulpbronne

Elke maatskappy wil voldoenend bly, en om by regeringsregulasies te hou kan dikwels kontantvloei en hulpbronne uitdun. Daar is geen nodigheid om fondse te bestee aan die aankoop & onderhoud van rekeningkundige sagteware wanneer jy kies om loonadministrasie uit te kontrakteer nie.

Rekeningkundige sagteware help met belastingvoldoenende fakture

Van ontwerp af stel die gebruik van rekeningkundige sagteware ’n groter mate van voldoening moontlik. Geoutomatiseerde tegnologie het die vermoë om kwotasies na fakture om te skakel.

Vir maatskappye met groot verkoopsvolumes en oorsese kliënte is die voordeel van die uitkontraktering van loonadministrasie aan firmas wat vertroud is met wette rakende elektroniese fakturering en die nuutste sagteware ooglopend. Nievoldoeningsboetes vir ongeldige fakture is ’n las wat niemand tydens ouditte wil hê nie. Sulke sagteware maak hierdie probleem ongedaan, terwyl dit skeduleringskonflikte oplos.

Bespaar jou tyd

Produktiwiteit klop besigheid, en as jy tyd aan bykomende take bestee, dan word jou tyd en geld grootliks onderbenut. Elke maatskappy behoort die buigsaamheid te hê om hul pogings op die regte plek te plaas en kan dit bekostig wanneer hulle loonadministrasiedienste uitkontrakteer.

Geen opleiding nie! Minder administrasie! 

Behalwe vir opleidingskoste neem die tyd wat dit verg om werknemers se vaardighede te verhoog van werksure af weg en neem dit later tyd in hul skedule op. Uitgekontrakteerde rekenmeesterfirmas bring loonadministrasiedienste saam met geoutomatiseerde sagteware, en dus ’n vinniger faktureringsproses oor die algemeen. Geen nodigheid om op betalingsadministrasie te wag nie. Enige redes om nie loonadministrasiedienste uit te kontrakteer nie behoort te verdwyn.

Veilig & vertroulik

Wanneer jy jou loonadministrasie opstel, voeg die kommer oor sekuriteit by die swaar proses. Eenmalige sekuriteitsmaatreëls kan lei tot ’n algehele langtermyn-ondermyning van die loonadministrasieproses, selfs al het jy daarin geslaag om die stelsel te outomatiseer. 

Minder risiko

 As jou maatskappy kies om loonadministrasie aan ’n rekenmeesterfirma uit te kontrakteer, verminder hulle ook risiko’s. Rekenmeesterfirmas is bewus van hierdie verantwoordelikheid en vermy enige kans van foutiewe betalings. Aangesien die betalings en begunstigdes aan ’n eksterne maatskappy behoort, gee hulle aandag daaraan om besonderhede by elke deel van die proses te bevestig. Elke salaris wat betaal word, word met maatreëls hanteer om te verseker dat daar geen verwarrings is nie.   

Terwyl daar talle redes is om loonadministrasie uit te kontrakteer, bly die feit staan dat so ’n noodsaaklike diens deur professionele rekenmeesters hanteer behoort te word. As jou maatskappy tyd, toegang tot rekeningkundige sagteware nodig het of beoog om op koste te bespaar, behoort die eerste taak wat oorweeg word loonadministrasiedienste te wees. Om hierdie taak uit jou rekenmeester se hande te neem verlig stres en gee jou ’n groter mate van voldoening ten opsigte van e-Indiening.

posts.title: Making sure your invoices comply with the VAT act
posts.excerpt: Value Added Tax, or VAT, is a standard tax that is applied indirectly to goods and services within an economy. In South Africa, the rate is 15% as of March 2023. Non-compliance with the VAT act could result in administrative fines and spillover effects that not only impact existing partnerships but also future business opportunities and relationships. ... Read more
posts.desc: Non-compliance with the VAT act could result in administrative fines & spillover effects. Read more.
posts.html:
Value Added Tax, or VAT, is a standard tax that is applied indirectly to goods and services within an economy. In South Africa, the rate is 15% as of March 2023. Non-compliance with the VAT act could result in administrative fines and spillover effects that not only impact existing partnerships but also future business opportunities and relationships. Having the correct knowledge helps you avoid such risks.

Do I have to register for VAT?

In South Africa, companies fall into two categories when it comes to VAT, one is compulsory and the other voluntary.

It is compulsory to register for and thus charge VAT if your business’s turnover exceeds or expects to exceed the local threshold of R1 million within twelve months. If that is the case, then your business may issue VAT invoices, unless of course your goods or services are zero-rated.

When your business’s turnover exceeds or expects to exceed R50k in a twelve-month period, then you have the option of charging VAT. Once you choose to charge, you are under an obligation to continually comply with the changes to VAT laws in the country.

If you do charge VAT while doing business, you also have the option to claim it back.

Full tax vs. Abridged tax invoice?

There are two main types of tax invoices; a full tax invoice and an abridged tax invoice. There are also your usual invoices (but that’s not discussed in the scope of this blog). So, what is the difference between the two?

Full tax invoice

A full tax invoice includes any amount charged over R5000 for goods and services. For the most part, companies with many purchases within the country and overseas issue these types of invoices. SARS has regulations set specifically for these instances.  

Abridged tax invoice

Any invoices with amounts ranging from R50 to R5000 will fall under an abridged tax invoice. In other countries, they may be split into two categories with different price brackets such as the UK.

For an abridged invoice, there are a few exemptions, mainly the details of the vendor as well as the number of goods or services supplied, which will be discussed soon.

What to include on the invoice

For a full tax invoice, whether it be electronic or on paper, all of the below points are required by SARS regulation. When it comes to an abridged invoice, points 3 & 6 are not required, but can however be included.

Here are the seven requirements for VAT-related invoices:

  • 1.      Details of the supplier
  • 2.     ‘Invoice’
  • 3.      Details of the customer
  • 4.      Invoice serial number and date of issue
  • 5.      Description of goods and services supplied
  • 6.      Quantity of the goods and services supplied
  • 7.      Overall value of the supply

Details of the supplier

Your business details would be crucial, regardless of regulation. SARS requires you to include your VAT registration number as well as the name and address of your business.

‘Invoice’

The specific word choice of ‘tax invoice’, ‘VAT invoice’, or simply ‘invoice’ must be included on the invoice.    

Details of customer

Whether it be a vendor or a private company, their details are also required. This includes, beyond their name and address, their VAT identification which allows one to reclaim VAT.

Invoice serial number and date of issue

This helps you keep track of the invoices; it is also advised to keep a copy in your books. All dates correspond to the date of issue of the invoice. What you should not forget to include are the invoices that have been cancelled, refunded, etc. There are record keeping guidelines that you should adhere to so as not to worry when you are audited later.

Description of goods and services supplied

You must write an accurate description of the supplies, and whether or not they are second-hand items. Having a good order and naming convention to go along with this helps you keep track of your stock, and prevents confusion down the line.

Quantity of the goods and services supplied

Usually found in the column alongside the description is the amount. This is used to then calculate the total amount owed to your business.

Overall value of the supply

This value must take the overall consideration of both the inclusive and exclusive tax amounts.  The total cost can be displayed after the description and amount of goods, while the VAT amount is mentioned elsewhere on the form.

Takeaways

Trading companies are required to comply with the SARS compliance checklist for VAT-related invoices. Whether or not they must register for VAT depends on their taxable income threshold.

When issuing tax invoices, they must either comply with all regulations in a full tax invoice, or partially with an abridged invoice. Including these points in a professional invoice keeps you compliant. This allows everything to stay hassle-free during the auditing process. It also maintains the respect and trust of your customers.

af.title: Maak seker jou fakture voldoen aan die BTW-wet
af.desc: Nievoldoening aan die BTW-wet kan administratiewe boetes & oorloopeffekte tot gevolg hê. Lees meer.
af.html:
Belasting op Toegevoegde Waarde, of BTW, is ’n standaard belasting wat indirek op goedere en dienste binne ’n ekonomie toegepas word. In Suid-Afrika is die koers 15% vanaf Maart 2023. Nievoldoening aan die BTW-wet kan administratiewe boetes en oorloopeffekte tot gevolg hê wat nie net bestaande vennootskappe raak nie, maar ook toekomstige besigheidsgeleenthede en verhoudings. Om die korrekte kennis te hê help jou om sulke risiko’s te vermy.

Moet ek vir BTW registreer?

In Suid-Afrika val maatskappye in twee kategorieë wanneer dit by BTW kom, een is verpligtend en die ander vrywillig.

Dit is verpligtend om te registreer en dus BTW te hef indien jou besigheid se omset die plaaslike drempel van R1 miljoen binne twaalf maande oorskry of verwag om te oorskry. Indien dit die geval is, mag jou besigheid BTW-fakture uitreik, tensy jou goedere of dienste natuurlik nulkoers is.

Wanneer jou besigheid se omset R50k in ’n tydperk van twaalf maande oorskry of verwag om te oorskry, dan het jy die opsie om BTW te hef. Sodra jy kies om te hef, is jy onder ’n verpligting om voortdurend te voldoen aan die veranderinge in die BTW-wette in die land.

As jy wel BTW hef terwyl jy besigheid doen, het jy ook die opsie om dit terug te eis.

Volledige belastingfaktuur vs. verkorte belastingfaktuur?

Daar is twee hooftipes belastingfakture; ’n volledige belastingfaktuur en ’n verkorte belastingfaktuur. Daar is ook jou gewone fakture (maar dit word nie binne die bestek van hierdie blog bespreek nie). So, wat is die verskil tussen die twee?

Volledige belastingfaktuur

’n Volledige belastingfaktuur sluit enige bedrag in wat oor R5000 vir goedere en dienste gehef word. Vir die grootste deel reik maatskappye met baie aankope binne die land en oorsee hierdie tipes fakture uit. SARS het regulasies wat spesifiek vir hierdie gevalle vasgestel is.  

Verkorte belastingfaktuur

Enige fakture met bedrae wat wissel van R50 tot R5000 sal onder ’n verkorte belastingfaktuur val. In ander lande mag hulle in twee kategorieë met verskillende prysgroepe verdeel word, soos die VK.

Vir ’n verkorte faktuur is daar ’n paar vrystellings, hoofsaaklik die besonderhede van die verkoper sowel as die hoeveelheid goedere of dienste wat verskaf word, wat binnekort bespreek sal word.

Wat om op die faktuur in te sluit

Vir ’n volledige belastingfaktuur, of dit elektronies of op papier is, word al die onderstaande punte deur SARS-regulasie vereis. Wanneer dit by ’n verkorte faktuur kom, word punte 3 & 6 nie vereis nie, maar kan wel ingesluit word.

Hier is die sewe vereistes vir BTW-verwante fakture:

  • 1.      Besonderhede van die verskaffer
  • 2. ‘Faktuur’
  • 3.      Besonderhede van die kliënt
  • 4.      Faktuur-reeksnommer en datum van uitreiking
  • 5.      Beskrywing van goedere en dienste verskaf
  • 6.      Hoeveelheid van die goedere en dienste verskaf
  • 7.      Algehele waarde van die lewering

Besonderhede van die verskaffer

Jou besigheidsbesonderhede sou noodsaaklik wees, ongeag regulasie. SARS vereis dat jy jou BTW-registrasienommer sowel as die naam en adres van jou besigheid insluit.

‘Faktuur’

Die spesifieke woordkeuse van ‘belastingfaktuur’, ‘BTW-faktuur’, of bloot ‘faktuur’ moet op die faktuur ingesluit word.    

Besonderhede van kliënt

Of dit ’n verkoper of ’n private maatskappy is, hul besonderhede word ook vereis. Dit sluit, behalwe hul naam en adres, hul BTW-identifikasie in wat ’n mens toelaat om BTW terug te eis.

Faktuur-reeksnommer en datum van uitreiking

Dit help jou om die fakture by te hou; dit word ook aangeraai om ’n afskrif in jou boeke te hou. Alle datums stem ooreen met die datum van uitreiking van die faktuur. Wat jy nie moet vergeet om in te sluit nie, is die fakture wat gekanselleer, terugbetaal, ens. is. Daar is rekordhoudingriglyne wat jy behoort na te kom sodat jy nie hoef te bekommer wanneer jy later geóudit word nie.

Beskrywing van goedere en dienste verskaf

Jy moet ’n akkurate beskrywing van die leweringe skryf, en of hulle tweedehandse items is al dan nie. Om ’n goeie orde en naamgewingskonvensie hiermee saam te hê help jou om jou voorraad by te hou, en voorkom verwarring later.

Hoeveelheid van die goedere en dienste verskaf

Gewoonlik gevind in die kolom langs die beskrywing is die hoeveelheid. Dit word dan gebruik om die totale bedrag verskuldig aan jou besigheid te bereken.

Algehele waarde van die lewering

Hierdie waarde moet die algehele oorweging van beide die belasting-ingeslote en belasting-uitgeslote bedrae in ag neem.  Die totale koste kan na die beskrywing en hoeveelheid goedere vertoon word, terwyl die BTW-bedrag elders op die vorm genoem word.

Wegneemboodskappe

Handelsmaatskappye word vereis om te voldoen aan die SARS-voldoeningskontrolelys vir BTW-verwante fakture. Of hulle vir BTW moet registreer al dan nie hang af van hul belasbare inkomstedrempel.

Wanneer hulle belastingfakture uitreik, moet hulle óf voldoen aan alle regulasies in ’n volledige belastingfaktuur, óf gedeeltelik met ’n verkorte faktuur. Om hierdie punte in ’n professionele faktuur in te sluit hou jou voldoenend. Dit laat alles probleemloos bly tydens die ouditproses. Dit handhaaf ook die respek en vertroue van jou kliënte.

posts.title: Demystifying tax for small businesses in South Africa
posts.excerpt: Paying tax for small businesses in South Africa may seem like a daunting task, but once you are equipped with the knowledge, you will think otherwise. Once your small business exceeds certain tax thresholds, SARS treats your business tax as one and the same as everyone else, there are no exceptions. It is better to ... Read more
posts.desc: All businesses are required by law to pay taxes and maintain a record for cases of tax returns. Learn more about tax for small businesses.
posts.html:
